stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 15 observations with mean 59.5 % (within-factory SD = 3.4). factory B has 8 observations with mean 55.4 % (within-factory SD = 9.0). factory C has 10 observations with mean 69.8 % (within-factory SD = 8.7). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.4 and 8.4 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory C: 68.2 % (shrunk from raw 69.8). Range [66.6, 69.0] as between-factory SD varies from 3.4 to 8.4. Pooled mean: 61.6.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 61.6 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=5.6: B = 0.806.
Interval [66.6, 69.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (69.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (61.6) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
